rationale: UTEP's CTIS has 20+ years of transportation infrastructure research directly applicable to border freight optimization. Hunt Institute provides binational trade data. AAII can develop predictive models for border crossing delays. DOT Smart Freight pilot is the highest-fit federal program for this problem.
partnershipStructure: UTEP (lead) + City of El Paso + TxDOT + CBP + Borderplex Alliance
gaps: Requires binational coordination framework with Mexico

rationale: UTEP's Center for Inland Desalination Systems is world-class in arid water treatment. CERM provides environmental monitoring. Rural communities qualify for USDA Rural Development and EPA Border Water programs—high-probability awards given colonia designation.
partnershipStructure: El Paso County (lead applicant) + UTEP (technical partner) + El Paso Water Utilities + local water districts
gaps: Require community engagement for needs assessment

rationale: UTEP is uniquely positioned as a binational convener for aquifer management—CIDS for desalination technology, CERM for monitoring, CIABS for binational governance research. NADB/BECC funding is specifically designed for this type of cross-border environmental challenge.
partnershipStructure: UTEP (convener) + El Paso Water + Ciudad Juárez water authority + IBWC + NADB

rationale: The Borderplex workforce misalignment problem is precisely what NSF Regional Innovation Engines targets. UTEP can convene a cross-sector coalition combining Hunt Institute economic analysis, Loya Center entrepreneurship programs, AAII for AI-enabled skills matching, and Borderplex Alliance employer connections.
partnershipStructure: UTEP (convener) + NMSU + Borderplex Alliance + MVEDA + City of EP + Las Cruces + 10+ industry partners
gaps: Need preliminary data on regional technology cluster

rationale: UTEP's cybersecurity, advanced manufacturing, and microelectronics capabilities directly serve defense workforce needs. Keck Center's AM capabilities, RIMES manufacturing research, and MNF cleanroom align with DOD Manufacturing Communities priorities. Proximity to Fort Bliss and WSMR strengthens the case.
partnershipStructure: UTEP (lead) + Fort Bliss + WSMR + Raytheon + Lockheed Martin + NMSU
gaps: Need formal MOUs with defense installations

rationale: BBRC's NIH-funded border health disparities research and CIABS' community health expertise directly address the HPSA designation. HRSA workforce programs fund community health worker training. CPRIT adds cancer prevention research funding.
partnershipStructure: UTEP (lead) + TTUHSC El Paso + El Paso County Health Dept + community health centers

rationale: The Borderplex's binational manufacturing ecosystem (Juárez maquiladoras + UTEP engineering capabilities) creates a unique EDA Tech Hub pitch. Keck Center advanced manufacturing + RIMES automation + Hunt Institute trade data + AAII for AI-enabled quality control. No other region combines US university R&D with the scale of cross-border manufacturing.
partnershipStructure: UTEP (lead) + Foxconn + Flex + BRP + Bosch + Borderplex Alliance + MVEDA

rationale: UTEP's CIDS and CERM address the water-energy nexus central to Las Cruces' renewable transition. DOE efficiency grants and NM Water Trust Fund are well-aligned funding sources. NMSU as a co-lead leverages NM eligibility.
partnershipStructure: NMSU (co-lead) + UTEP (co-PI) + City of Las Cruces + El Paso Electric + NM PRC
gaps: Las Cruces grid modernization plan required

rationale: UTEP's Micro and Nanofabrication Facility + Keck Center + RIMES create a semiconductor workforce and R&D cluster. Texas Semiconductor Innovation Fund is purpose-built for this. NSF Broadening Participation adds diversity pipeline funding.
partnershipStructure: UTEP (lead) + TX Higher Ed Coordinating Board + industry partners
gaps: MNF needs equipment upgrades for current-gen processes

rationale: AAII's data analytics can map underserved areas and optimize deployment strategies. Cyber-SHARE ensures cybersecurity for rural broadband. BEAD funding flows through state broadband offices—UTEP can serve as technical advisor to communities applying for subgrants.
partnershipStructure: TX Broadband Development Office or NM Connect (lead) + El Paso County + Doña Ana County + UTEP (technical advisor)
gaps: Need relationship with state broadband offices

rationale: Proximity to Sandia and LANL creates a quantum corridor opportunity. UTEP's Cyber-SHARE and MNF provide foundation. NSF Engines could fund a quantum technology regional ecosystem. DOE lab partnership programs enable CRADA pathways.
gaps: Need formal national lab partnership agreements

rationale: UTEP Aerospace Center + CASSMAR + Spaceport America partnership creates a space technology corridor. NASA Space Technology grants and AFWERX programs fund university aerospace R&D. NMSU aerospace engineering adds depth.
partnershipStructure: UTEP (co-PI) + NMSU + Spaceport America + Blue Origin + NASA WSTF

rationale: Hunt Institute provides housing market analysis and policy research. CIABS adds community engagement expertise. HUD CDBG is a natural funding source for both cities. EDA adjustment assistance supports housing-linked economic development.
partnershipStructure: City of El Paso (lead) + City of Las Cruces + UTEP (research partner) + housing developers

rationale: CTIS can assess infrastructure needs and design solutions. CERM provides environmental compliance support. Socorro qualifies for USDA Rural, HUD CDBG, and EDA grants. UTEP plays technical advisor role.
partnershipStructure: City of Socorro (lead applicant) + UTEP (technical advisor) + El Paso County

rationale: Loya Center's incubation programs + AAII's tech capabilities + Hunt Institute's economic analysis create an entrepreneurship ecosystem. SBIR/STTR and EDA grants support startup formation and innovation infrastructure.
partnershipStructure: UTEP (coordinator) + Loya Center + NMSU Arrowhead Center + Borderplex Alliance + local startups
gaps: Need inventory of existing startup support programs
